claim_text: On July 31, 2025, the president established a new tariff structure: 10 percent tariffs on countries with which the US runs a surplus, 15 percent tariffs on countries with which it has a small deficit, and higher tariffs for countries with which the US has large trade deficits.
subject: Multiple countries based on trade balance status
certainty_rationale: The claim describes a specific tariff structure established by the president on a named date, sourced from official USTR press materials, which constitutes a formal government announcement but lacks evidence of signed executive order documentation or Federal Register publication.

claim_text: The US Supreme Court ruled 6-3 on February 20, 2026, that IEEPA (International Emergency Economic Powers Act) does not authorize the President to impose tariffs, invalidating all tariffs issued under that authority including reciprocal tariffs and fentanyl-related tariffs on China, Canada, and Mexico.
subject: China, Canada, Mexico
certainty_rationale: Supreme Court decision (Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump, 24-1287) is binding law of the land.

claim_text: Following the Supreme Court IEEPA ruling, President Trump imposed a 10 percent ad valorem import surcharge on nearly all imported goods under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, effective February 24, 2026, for a 150-day period ending July 24, 2026.
subject: China, Canada, Mexico, EU, European Union, India, Vietnam, Japan, South Korea, Brazil, Turkey, Germany
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation signed invoking Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 with official White House fact sheet.

claim_text: President Trump signaled plans to increase the Section 122 import surcharge from 10 percent to the statutory maximum of 15 percent via a Truth Social post on February 21, 2026, though no formal action has yet been taken as of early March 2026.
subject: China, Canada, Mexico, EU, European Union, India, Vietnam, Japan, South Korea, Brazil, Turkey
certainty_rationale: Announced via social media post only; no executive order or proclamation issued as of early March 2026.

claim_text: Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum at 50 percent (25 percent for UK-origin goods) remain in effect for nearly all trading partners and are explicitly exempt from the Section 122 surcharge, as they were not invalidated by the Supreme Court IEEPA ruling.
subject: China, Canada, Mexico, EU, European Union, India, Brazil, Turkey, South Korea, Japan, Germany, United Kingdom
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962; confirmed still in effect after SCOTUS IEEPA ruling.

claim_text: Canada faces a 35 percent tariff on non-USMCA-compliant goods effective March 4, 2025, with USMCA-compliant imports currently exempt from both that tariff and the new Section 122 surcharge. Auto imports were separately exempted until April 2, 2026.
subject: Canada
certainty_rationale: Tariff imposed via executive order under IEEPA in early 2025; rate and USMCA exemption confirmed through subsequent proclamations.

claim_text: Mexico faces a 25 percent tariff on non-USMCA-compliant goods effective March 4, 2025. USMCA-compliant imports remain exempt from the tariff, with exemptions extended indefinitely following an April 2 agreement.
subject: Mexico
certainty_rationale: Tariff imposed via executive order; USMCA exemption confirmed through subsequent presidential proclamation.

claim_text: China faces a total effective tariff of approximately 35 percent on most goods as of early March 2026, comprised of the Section 122 global 10 percent surcharge stacked with surviving Section 301 tariffs (7.5-25 percent on most goods) and a 10 percent fentanyl-related tariff reduced from 20 percent following a November 2025 agreement.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Multiple overlapping tariff layers confirmed by official proclamations and USTR; fentanyl rate reduction agreed November 2025.

claim_text: The US and India announced an interim bilateral trade deal on February 2, 2026, reducing the US reciprocal tariff on India from 50 percent to 18 percent, after India agreed to stop purchasing Russian oil and commit to $500 billion in US product purchases over five years.
subject: India
certainty_rationale: Formalized by executive order on February 6, 2026; confirmed by White House fact sheet and joint statement.

claim_text: Vietnam faces a 20 percent tariff on general imports with a 40 percent tariff on goods determined to be transshipped through Vietnam from third countries, following a reduction from the original 46 percent reciprocal rate announced in late July 2025.
subject: Vietnam
certainty_rationale: Announced by Trump administration and confirmed in tariff tracker data; rate reduction from 46% to 20% officially stated.

claim_text: A 25 percent Section 232 tariff on imported automobiles took effect April 3, 2025, with modified rates for some partners. Auto parts face a 25 percent tariff effective May 3, 2025, with USMCA-compliant auto parts exempt.
subject: China, EU, European Union, Japan, South Korea, Canada, Mexico, Germany
certainty_rationale: Section 232 Presidential Proclamation; not invalidated by SCOTUS IEEPA ruling as it rests on different statutory authority.

claim_text: A 25 percent Section 232 tariff on advanced semiconductors (specific logic circuits) took effect January 15, 2026, targeting chips manufactured in China and other non-allied nations, with multiple product-specific exemptions.
subject: China, Taiwan, South Korea
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation under Section 232; described with specific HTSUS codes and exemptions in official White House documentation.

claim_text: A Section 232 investigation into commercial aircraft, jet engines, and parts was initiated on May 1, 2025. The investigation, which could result in tariffs affecting Canada, EU/Germany, Japan, and Brazil (major aircraft exporters), was required to conclude by approximately January 26, 2026, with tariffs considered likely.
subject: Canada, EU, European Union, Germany, Japan, Brazil
certainty_rationale: Official investigation opened under Section 232; tariff outcome not yet formally announced as of early March 2026.

claim_text: A Section 232 investigation into pharmaceuticals and pharmaceutical ingredients (active pharmaceutical ingredients/APIs) was initiated on April 1, 2025, with the potential to impose up to 100 percent tariffs on branded/patented products from countries including India, China, Germany, and the EU.
subject: China, India, EU, European Union, Germany
certainty_rationale: Official Section 232 investigation opened with 100 percent tariff threatened for branded pharmaceuticals; final rule not yet published.

claim_text: A 50 percent Section 232 tariff on copper and semi-finished copper products took effect August 1, 2025, calculated on the copper content of affected imports from all countries including Chile, Canada, Mexico, and China.
subject: China, Canada, Mexico
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation under Section 232; effective date confirmed in official tracker data.

claim_text: A 10 percent Section 232 tariff on softwood lumber took effect October 14, 2025, primarily affecting Canada which supplies over 80 percent of US softwood lumber imports, stacking with existing anti-dumping and countervailing duties.
subject: Canada
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation under Section 232 with specific HTSUS codes confirmed in official tracker.

claim_text: Following the SCOTUS IEEPA ruling, the Trump administration announced plans to open new Section 301 investigations as a tool to replace invalidated IEEPA tariffs, potentially targeting China, Vietnam, Cambodia, India, and other countries for unfair trade practices.
subject: China, Vietnam, Cambodia, India
certainty_rationale: Administration previewed new Section 301 investigations publicly but no formal Federal Register notice published as of early March 2026.

claim_text: Mexico imposed broad new tariffs of up to 35 percent on 1,400 products from China and other Asian countries lacking free trade agreements with Mexico, effective January 2026, to curb the use of Mexico as a transshipment route to avoid US-China tariffs.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Official Mexican government action announced and implemented, reported across multiple trade publications.

claim_text: Section 122 tariffs explicitly exempt USMCA duty-free goods, civil aircraft articles, goods already subject to Section 232 tariffs, and products in Annex II (certain agricultural products, minerals, and energy), providing relief to Canada, Mexico and specific sectors globally.
subject: Canada, Mexico
certainty_rationale: Exemptions explicitly listed in the Section 122 Presidential Proclamation signed February 20, 2026.

claim_text: Section 232 tariffs impose 25 percent on all steel imports and 10 percent on all aluminum imports from most countries, including China, Canada, Mexico, EU, Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, India, Brazil, Turkey, and Germany.
subject: China, Canada, Mexico, EU, European Union, Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, India, Brazil, Turkey, Germany

claim_text: Executive order imposes 25 percent tariffs on all goods from Canada, effective March 4, 2025, citing national security and fentanyl concerns under IEEPA.
subject: Canada

claim_text: Executive order imposes 25 percent tariffs on all goods from Mexico, effective March 4, 2025, citing national security and fentanyl concerns under IEEPA.
subject: Mexico

claim_text: Cumulative executive orders impose an additional 20 percent tariff on all Chinese goods — 10 percent effective February 4, 2025 and an additional 10 percent effective March 4, 2025 — on top of existing Section 301 tariffs averaging 25 percent.
subject: China

claim_text: Section 301 tariffs impose 25 percent on Chinese electronics, semiconductors, chips, laptops, tablets, and consumer electronics, as enacted under the USMCA-successor trade framework.
subject: China

claim_text: Section 301 tariffs impose 25 percent on Chinese apparel, clothing, garments, footwear, and handbags under List 3 and List 4A tariffs.
subject: China

claim_text: Biden administration solar panel tariffs impose 50 percent on Chinese solar panels, photovoltaic cells, and solar energy equipment, effective from 2024.
subject: China

claim_text: Biden administration EV tariffs impose 100 percent on Chinese electric vehicles, effective from 2024.
subject: China

claim_text: Section 301 investigation into Vietnam's currency practices; meanwhile, Most Favored Nation rates average 17 percent on Vietnamese apparel, clothing, and garments.
subject: Vietnam

claim_text: Bangladesh apparel and garment exports face standard Most Favored Nation tariff rates averaging 12 percent, with no additional special tariffs imposed.
subject: Bangladesh

claim_text: India apparel, clothing, and footwear exports to the US face standard MFN tariff rates of 12 percent on average; no additional tariffs imposed.
subject: India

claim_text: Indian steel imports subject to 25 percent Section 232 tariffs; no quota exemption arrangement in place.
subject: India

claim_text: EU and European Union steel and aluminum exports subject to 25 percent Section 232 tariffs; Section 232 suspension lifted and tariffs reinstated after failed quota arrangement.
subject: EU, European Union, Germany

claim_text: Japanese steel imports subject to 25 percent Section 232 tariffs; Japan reached tariff-rate quota arrangement but it remains subject to 25 percent above quota levels.
subject: Japan

claim_text: South Korean auto and vehicle imports face 25 percent Section 232 tariffs as imposed by executive order on automobile imports.
subject: South Korea

claim_text: Executive order imposes 25 percent tariffs on all imported automobiles, vehicles, cars, trucks, and automobile parts, effective April 3, 2025.
subject: China, Japan, South Korea, Germany, EU, European Union, Canada, Mexico

claim_text: Cambodia faces 49 percent reciprocal tariff on apparel, clothing, garments, and textile exports to the US, announced in the April 2025 reciprocal tariff executive order.
subject: Cambodia

claim_text: Vietnam faces 46 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including apparel, clothing, footwear, and electronics, announced under April 2025 reciprocal tariff executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Vietnam

claim_text: Bangladesh faces 37 percent reciprocal tariff on apparel, clothing, and garments under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline tariff during 90-day negotiation window.
subject: Bangladesh

claim_text: Indonesia faces 32 percent reciprocal tariff on apparel, clothing, garments, footwear, and electronics under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Indonesia

claim_text: Thailand faces 36 percent reciprocal tariff on apparel, clothing, electronics, and machinery under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Thailand

claim_text: India faces 26 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline during 90-day negotiation window.
subject: India

claim_text: Japan faces 24 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including auto, vehicles, electronics, and machinery under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Japan

claim_text: The European Union faces 20 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including chemicals, machinery, auto, and agriculture under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: EU, European Union, Germany

claim_text: South Korea faces 25 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including electronics, semiconductors, auto, and machinery under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: South Korea

claim_text: Taiwan faces 32 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including semiconductors, electronics, and machinery under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Taiwan

claim_text: Malaysia faces 24 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including electronics, apparel, and chemicals under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Malaysia

claim_text: Pakistan faces 29 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including apparel, textiles, and clothing under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Pakistan

claim_text: Sri Lanka faces 44 percent reciprocal tariff on all goods including apparel, clothing, and garments under April 2025 executive order, currently paused at 10 percent baseline.
subject: Sri Lanka

claim_text: China reciprocal tariff escalated to 145 percent total on all Chinese goods (including Section 301 and IEEPA tariffs combined), after China retaliated and the 90-day pause was not extended to China.
subject: China

claim_text: Universal baseline tariff of 10 percent applied to all countries under April 2025 executive order as a floor rate; some country-specific higher rates apply on top.
subject: China, Vietnam, Mexico, Bangladesh, India, Cambodia, Indonesia, Thailand, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Turkey, Brazil, South Korea, Taiwan, Japan, Germany, Canada, Malaysia, Philippines
